chapter.label: Tale 56
chapter.title: The Life Of John, The Disciple Of A Great Elder Who Lived In The Village Of Parasema

paragraphs.text: Prolemais is a city of Phoenicia. There is a village nearby called Paraséma in which there resided a greal elder. He had a disciple named John who was also great and who excelled in obedience. One day the elder seat the disciple to perform a task for him, giving him a litle bread to sustain him on the way. The disciple went and completed the task and then came back, bringing the bread with him, untouched. When the elder saw the bread, he said to him: ‘Why did you not eat any of the bread 1 gave you, my child?

Making an act of obeisance, the disciple said to the elder: ‘Forgive me, father, but when you blessed me and dismissed me, you did not say I was to eat of the bread; and that is why I did not eat it".
Amazed at the disciple’s discernment, the elder gave him his blessing. After the death of this elder, a vision from God appeared

{6 the brother (who had just concluded a forty-day last) which said to him: ‘Whatever disorder you lay your hand on, it shall be healed’. When morning came, by the providence of God a man arrived, bringing his wife who liad a cancer of the breast. The man besought the brother to heal his wife, The brother replied: ‘I am a sinful nan and unworthy of such an undertaking’. The woman's husband continued to beg him to accede to his request and to have pity on his wife. So the brother laid his hand on the diseased part and sealed it with the sign of the cross and she was immediately healed. From that time on God performed many signs through him, nat only in his own lifetime, but also after his death.*
57. THE DEATH OF SYMEON THE STYLITE* AND CONCERNING ABBA JULIAN, ANOTHER STYLITE

Four miles fram the city of Agaion a stylite was installed whose name was Symeon. He was struck by lightening and died. Abba Julian the stylite whose pillar was on the Gull of Alexandretta? told his disciples to burn incense, not at the accustomed time. The disciples said to him: “Tell us why, father’. He said to them: ‘Because brother Symeon of /Egaion has been struck by lightening and is dead. Behold, his soul is departing with great gladness’. They were twenty-four miles (rom each other.”
